title: Tabernaemontana stenoptera
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana stenoptera is classified within the Apocynaceae family, which includes over 5,000 species of plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is further divided into 346 genera, with Tabernaemontana being one of them. Tabernaemontana stenoptera belongs to the genus Tabernaemontana and is a species of flowering plant native to South America. The species is also known as the yellow-coral tree due to its bright yellow flowers. Its classification is essential in understanding the evolutionary history and relationships of this species within the plant kingdom.
uses: Tabernaemontana stenoptera is reported to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and as an antiseptic. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

intro: Tabernaemontana stenoptera, also known as the "Wild Jasmine" or "Quebra-Pedra", is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana stenoptera include alkaloids such as tabernaemontanine and stenoptrine, which are believed to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make tea, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, as well as a tincture and a resin, which are used to treat pain and inflammation.
